pattern: Buffer arrivals by their slot number and only release the growing prefix once every slot up to the current cursor has been filled.
hints: You don't need to re-scan everything on every call — track the single boundary between what has already been released and what hasn't.
Store each arriving value at its own slot in a fixed-size array, then look at whether the very next unreleased slot is now filled.
After writing a slot, walk forward from the cursor collecting a contiguous run of filled slots, advancing the cursor past each one you collect; stop the moment you hit an empty slot.

edge_inputs.description: single-slot stream, one insert immediately flushes
inserts arrive in reverse order, nothing flushes until the last one completes the prefix
inserts arrive already in order, each flushes exactly one value
filling a middle gap flushes multiple buffered values at once
inserting the last id first must not flush anything until earlier ids arrive
statement-style example: five slots, out-of-order arrival with a partial flush then a full catch-up
n at the constraint maximum with only the first slot filled

stress.description: random permutation of slot arrivals over a mid-size stream, each id inserted exactly once
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    order = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(order)
    ops = ['OrderedStream']
    args = [[n]]
    alphabet = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    for idKey in order:
        value = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(5))
        ops.append('insert')
        args.append([idKey, value])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: each id appears exactly once in a shuffled order, matching the problem's actual usage pattern

oracle_python:
class OrderedStream:
    def __init__(self, n: int):
        self.n = n
        self.slots = [None] * (n + 1)
        self.next_needed = 1
    def insert(self, idKey: int, value: str):
        self.slots[idKey] = value
        res = []
        scan = self.next_needed
        while scan <= self.n and self.slots[scan] is not None:
            res.append(self.slots[scan])
            scan += 1
        self.next_needed = scan
        return res
